note: SYNTHESIZED from ST 1.18.0 source (public/scripts/openai.js:3827-3866 squashSystemMessages; gated at :1599-1601), not captured. Prose is RPT-authored + scrambled; no ST default utility strings appear. Pins `squash_system_messages: true`: consecutive UNNAMED `system` messages are merged with a single '\n' (openai.js:3846), and an EMPTY system message is force-dropped (openai.js:3835-3837) WITHOUT breaking the surrounding run — so main+secondary+tertiary+quaternary collapse into ONE system message across the dropped empty `spacer`. The two consecutive USER turns stay DISCRETE, proving squash is system-only (unlike RPT's merge-all `mergeConsecutiveRoles`, which native presets keep). ST's protected control identifiers (newMainChat/newChat/groupNudge, openai.js:3828) are ST-internal separators RPT does not emit; their survival is pinned by the providerShape unit test, not this fixture. Adapter-verify at Phase-2 wiring.

input.description: Four adjacent unnamed system literals with an empty `spacer` between the 3rd and 4th; two trailing consecutive user turns; squash on.
